title: Profile with hardware performance counters
description: Find the real bottleneck class (front-end, back-end, memory, branch) using PMU counters before changing any code.
body: Steps: 1. Build with optimizations and symbols, run on representative input. 2. Collect a top-down breakdown (perf stat / VTune): IPC, cache miss rates, branch mispredicts, stalls. 3. Classify the bottleneck — front-end bound, back-end/memory bound, bad speculation, or retiring. 4. Pick the matching strategy (locality, vectorize, fix branches) and change one thing. 5. Re-measure on the same input. Rollback point: before each code change, record the baseline counters. Validation: the targeted counter must move in the right direction and wall-clock must improve on representative data; a faster synthetic microbenchmark with worse counters is not progress.
